page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized virtual machine based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel processor cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The underlying Intel processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512), which accelerate matrix multiplication and vector operations. Benchmark data shows average performance across most compute workloads, with a PassMark CPU score of 12,045.57 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 7,215.0. Notably, it demonstrates strong performance in text processing with a Geekbench score of 2,404.0, placing it in the top 25% tier.

Memory performance is a potential bottleneck, as indicated by a weak PassMark memory score of 1,718.22, though cached small-block read bandwidth reaches 568.81 GB/sec. Large-block uncached read and write bandwidths are 68.85 GB/sec and 40.33 GB/sec, respectively. For language model workloads, the instance handles small 135M parameter models efficiently, achieving 219.21 tokens/sec for text generation, but performance drops to a weak 7.92 tokens/sec on medium 7B models. This server is best suited for compute-bound tasks with moderate memory footprints, such as software compilation, text processing, static web serving, and lightweight database operations.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. Lacking bundled local storage or GPUs, this instance relies on network-attached resources. It features a large 260 MB L3 cache and supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. Performance benchmarks are generally average, highlighted by a PassMark CPU score of 12,045.57 and a strong Geekbench text processing score of 2,404.0. However, memory performance is relatively weak, as shown by a PassMark memory score of 1,718.22. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads with low memory requirements, making it suitable for software compilation, text processing, static web serving, and basic database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized VM with 8 Intel vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage. It features Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for compute-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized VM featuring 8 Intel vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and advanced Intel AMX instruction support.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel processors with advanced matrix extensions for accelerated vector and text processing workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support
Strong performance in single-threaded text processing benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and light databases
